tests.title: Director skips director processing for signal rules
Director rewrites event type to "alert" and populates episode id/status on every processed event
Director preserves the same episode id across pending -> active -> recovering -> inactive
Director generates a new episode id when a previously inactive group breaches again
Director uses the basic strategy when state_transition is omitted (no status_count is ever set)
Director increments status_count across consecutive pending executions and resets it on transition to active
Director starts a new pending lifecycle at status_count 1 after the previous lifecycle reached inactive
Director does not write status_count on inactive episode events
Director keeps episode in recovering until recovering_count threshold is met
Director recovering_count: 0 skips the recovering status and goes straight to inactive
Director pending_count: 0 skips the pending status and goes straight to active
Director transitions recovering -> active when the group breaches again
Director transitions pending -> inactive when a group recovers before the pending threshold is met
Director tracks episode id and status_count independently per group
Director transitions pending -> active via pending_timeframe even when pending_count is unreachable
Director transitions recovering -> inactive via recovering_timeframe when recovering_operator is OR
Director keeps the latest episode in recovering when only recovering_timeframe is met under recovering_operator AND
Director keeps the latest episode in pending when only pending_timeframe is met under pending_operator AND
Director transitions pending -> active under pending_operator AND when both count and timeframe are met
Dispatcher does not dispatch any episodes when there are no alert events
Dispatcher dispatches all unique episodes when alert events have no prior fire actions
Dispatcher persists notified actions for dispatched action groups when the action policy has a throttle interval
Dispatcher only dispatches the new events when some episodes already have fires
Dispatcher dispatches fire actions for non-suppressed episodes and suppress actions for suppressed ones when alert episodes have user actions
Dispatcher lifts suppression when the last user action is an unsnooze, an expired snooze, or an activate
Dispatcher only dispatches episodes matching the KQL expression when the action policy has a matcher
Dispatcher groups episodes by the specified data fields when the action policy has groupBy fields
Dispatcher throttle strategies / per_episode + on_status_change throttles on a second dispatch when status is unchanged
Dispatcher throttle strategies / per_episode + per_status_interval throttles within the interval when status is unchanged
Dispatcher throttle strategies / per_episode + every_time dispatches a new event even when the episode status is unchanged
Dispatcher throttle strategies / all + time_interval digest groups all episodes and stays throttled on subsequent dispatches
Dispatcher throttle strategies / per_field + time_interval throttles groups on subsequent dispatches within the interval
Dispatcher suppresses dispatch with `reason: maintenance_window:<id>` when an enabled maintenance window covers the alert events
Dispatcher single_rule policy / dispatches only for the linked rule and skips unrelated rules
Dispatcher multiple policies / produces one fire per matching policy when the same episode matches both
Dispatcher lookback window / does not dispatch events older than the dispatcher lookback window
Dispatcher snoozed policy / does not dispatch when the action policy is snoozed until a future time
Dispatcher missing destination / still records fire and notified actions when the destination workflow does not exist
Dispatcher writes a throttled-policy suppress record when on_status_change holds back an episode
Dispatcher writes an `unmatched` action record when no policy matches the rule
End-to-end (executor -> director -> dispatcher) a breaching rule produces a fire action via the full end-to-end
End-to-end (executor -> director -> dispatcher) a recovering rule produces a fire action for the inactive transition
Matcher data fields suggestions API returns the union of data.* field names across all matching alerts when no matcher is given
Matcher data fields suggestions API narrows fields when matcher selects a single rule via rule.id
Matcher data fields suggestions API falls back to the unfiltered result when matcher cannot be parsed
Matcher data fields suggestions API falls back to the unfiltered result when matcher only references dropped fields
Matcher data fields suggestions API validation: rejects empty matcher with a 400
Matcher data fields suggestions API validation: rejects matcher longer than the schema limit with a 400
Matcher data fields suggestions API authorization: returns 200 for a user with read-only privileges
Matcher data fields suggestions API authorization: returns 403 for a user without alerting_v2 privileges

Rule executor writes one breach event per matching ES|QL row
Rule executor writes the columns returned by the query to the data field correctly
Rule executor populates rule, source, and scheduling fields on every breach event
Rule executor writes one event per group when grouping.fields is set
Rule executor groups multiple documents into the same group correctly
Rule executor produces stable group_hash for the same group across executions
Rule executor produces the correct breach events for match based queries without grouping
Rule executor extracts severity from the ES|QL row onto breached events as a top-level field
Rule executor includes data within the lookback window even when older than schedule.every
Rule executor excludes data outside the lookback window
Rule executor defaults the lookback window to schedule.every when no lookback is set
Rule executor uses the configured time_field for the lookback range filter
Rule executor binds the ?_tstart and ?_tend reserved ES|QL params to the lookback window
Rule executor emits a recovered event under no_breach policy when a previously breaching group stops breaching
Rule executor does not emit recovery events for kind: "signal" rules when previously matching groups stop matching
Rule executor does not emit recovery events for alert rules created without a recovery_policy
Rule executor uses the configured query under recovery_policy.type=query
Rule executor emits one recovery event per active group when recovery_policy.type=query matches multiple
Rule executor does not recover a group when recovery_policy.type=query returns no matching active group
Rule executor does not recover a group when recovery_policy.type=query returns zero rows
Rule executor emits recovery events only for groups that stop breaching when others keep breaching
Rule executor emits both breach and recovery events in the same execution when one group recovers and another keeps breaching
Rule executor writes zero events when the ES|QL query returns no rows
Rule executor a disabled rule writes no new events
Rule executor resumes producing events after a disabled rule is re-enabled
Rule executor a deleted rule writes no new events
Rule executor an invalid ES|QL query writes no events
